MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF MICKEY QIU's Motion For Permission To Amend Response To Request For Admission.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Giorgio O. Panagos, Esq., of ULYSSES LAW, PC, (510) 220-1333, for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling. This motor vehicle accident case arises as a result of a collision between plaintiff riding a skateboard and a vehicle for which defendant Worldpac is responsible. The accident occurred on Polk Street on August 4, 2022. As this action was pending, defendants served plaintiff with discovery requests, one of which contained a request for admissions. In response to one request, plaintiff admitted to operating a "motorized skateboard" at the time of the accident. Subsequently, he claims he discovered that his admission implicated use limitations which significantly prejudiced his chance of prevailing on his complaint, use limitations he had never intended to deem applicable to the facts of this case. The court file does not contain any opposition to plaintiff's motion for leave to amend his response to Request for Admissions No. 10 to clarify that he does not admit to operating a "motorized skateboard" at the time of the accident. Instead, he wants to assert he was riding a "electronically motorized board" when the accident occurred. In the absence of any opposition by defendants, plaintiff's motion for leave to amend the subject admission is GRANTED. Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare a form of proposed order to submit to the Court, at psw@hassard.com, for review and execution.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)
